title: My Work — Darren Jones MP
headings: h2: My income and office costs:
text_excerpt: My Work — Darren Jones MP 0 Skip to Content Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Darren Jones MP Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Open Menu Close Menu Ask for help Policy questions For schools Events About me About Bristol North West Contact Subscribe to mailing list > Your Voice in Parliament. Your Champion in Bristol. Bristol North West is my home. That makes being the local MP extra special. In 2024, I was proudly re-elected to serve my constituents as the local Labour MP. As your Voice in Parliament, it’s my job to raise your concerns and to campaign for change. As your Champion in Bristol, it’s my job to support you, your family and our community to make Bristol the best place to live and work. My work in Westminster In addition to representing Bristol North West in the House of Commons, I am the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster. That means, in addition to my job as your Member of Parliament, I serve as a government minister and attend cabinet. The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister role is a new appointment, and I’ll be working collaboratively across government to drive forward progress in key policy areas –based within 10 Downing Street and reporting directly to the Prime Minister. The Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster leads the Cabinet Office, including oversight of all policy, national security, and matters pertaining to government ethics. Prior to these roles, I served as Chief Secretary to the Treasury, and before the 2024 General Election, I served in opposition as Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury. I was also previously Chair of the House of Commons Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Committee (which was later renamed the Business and Trade Committee). Darren’s biography. Darren was born at Southmead Hospital in the 1980s and grew up in Lawrence Weston in Bristol North West. He went to Avon Primary and Portway Community schools, now called Nova Primary and Oasis Academy Brightstowe. Darren continued his education at the South Bristol Post-16 Centre at Ashton Gate school before reading human bioscience at Plymouth University, with a focus on immunology. He was elected President of Plymouth Students’ Union, and went on to work for the NHS. Darren later trained as a solicitor, specialising in technology law. He worked in in Plymouth, Bristol and London in both private practice and as an in-house counsel, with a particular focus on the energy and telecommunications sectors. Darren first ran for Parliament in 2010 as a Labour candidate in the Conservative safe seat of Torridge and West Devon. He was selected in 2012 to contest the marginal seat of Bristol North West, moving the Labour Party from third to second place in the 2015 election. Darren was first elected to Parliament as the Member for Bristol North West in the 2017 election and was re-elected in both the 2019 and 2024 general elections. In 2024, Darren was appointed as Chief Secretary to the Treasury by the Prime Minister – a role that he held until he was appointed in 2025 as Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister. Alongside this role, he was also appointed to lead the Cabinet Office as Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster. As a member of the cabinet, he is a privy counsellor. Darren is married to Lucy and has three daughters. My income and office costs: You don’t just vote for your Member of Parliament, you pay for them too. That’s why I’ve always published my income and office costs on my website - so you can see everything with complete transparency. You can check individual costs by clicking the ‘office costs in detail’ button below.
